description: Animated 3D Fault Simulation Engine and UI for the SLD module. Implements fault injection, trip time calculation, animated visualization with 3D neon glow style, and results panel. Integrates into the existing SLD module with a Fault Simulation mode button.
steps: STEP 1 - Fault Simulation Calculation Engine: Add a function sldSimulateFault(tree, nodeId, faultType) that: (a) accepts faultType 'short_circuit' or 'earth_fault', (b) uses sldGetPath(tree, nodeId) to get the path from root to fault point, (c) uses sldCalcNodeIk(tree, nodeId) to get fault current at that point, (d) walks the path from fault point toward root to find which protection device trips FIRST by computing trip time for each device on the path using sldDeviceCurve() and interpolating the time at the computed fault current, (e) determines the required disconnection time per DS/HD 60364-4-41 (0.4s for final circuits <=32A at 230V, 5s for distribution/other), (f) checks if additional upstream devices also trip (selectivity failure), (g) returns a result object: {faultNodeId, faultType, ikFault, zsAtFault, path (array of nodeIds), trippingDeviceNodeId, tripTime, requiredTime, meetsStandard (bool), clause, selectivityIssues (array), verdict ('protected'|'failed'|'slow')}. Trip time calculation must be CONSERVATIVE: use tMax from device curve (worst-case trip time). For currents below the device's minimum operating threshold, report 'no_trip'.

STEP 2 - Trip Time Interpolation Helper: Add function sldGetTripTime(device, In, Ifault, settings) that takes a protection device, its In rating, the fault current, and optional MCCB settings, calls sldDeviceCurve to get the curve, then interpolates tMax at Ifault. Use log-log interpolation between curve points. If Ifault is below the first point's current, return Infinity (device won't trip). If above the last point, return the last tMax. This is the conservative approach for life-safety.

STEP 3 - Fault Simulation State and Mode Toggle: Add global state variables (sldFaultSimMode, sldFaultSimResult, sldFaultSimAnimating, sldFaultSimFrame) and a button in renderSLD() that toggles fault simulation mode. When mode is active, clicking a node in the SVG canvas triggers sldInjectFault(nodeId) instead of sldSelectNode(). Add fault type selection buttons (short-circuit / earth fault) visible when mode is active. Use tx() for all labels (da/en with _FA entries for fa).

STEP 4 - Animated 3D SVG Fault Visualization: Add function sldRenderFaultAnimation(tree, result) that creates an SVG visualization in the 3D style (dark background with radial gradient, neon glow filters). The visualization shows: (a) all nodes on the fault path rendered as isometric 3D boxes (matching build3D() style with polygon faces), (b) animated fault current flowing along cables from fault point to source using SVG <animate>/<animateMotion> or stroke-dashoffset animation, (c) current path intensity proportional to fault current (brighter glow = higher current), (d) the tripping device flashes/pulses with a distinct animation (e.g., red strobe), (e) color coding: green path if protection is adequate, red path if failed/too slow, (f) a lightning bolt icon at the fault injection point. Use CSS keyframe animations defined inline in a <style> within the SVG or as SVG animate elements.

STEP 5 - Results Panel: Add function sldRenderFaultResults(result) that renders a detailed results card showing: fault current Ik (kA), which device tripped (name + rating), trip time (ms), required disconnection time, pass/fail verdict with color coding, Zs at fault point (mohm), DS/HD 60364-4-41 clause citation, selectivity issues (list of upstream devices that also trip within the fault duration). Use the existing card/result-box CSS classes. Include trilingual labels.

STEP 6 - Integration into renderSLD(): Modify the renderSLD() function to include: (a) a 'Fault Simulation' mode button in the toolbar area, (b) when sldFaultSimMode is active, show fault type selection and instruction text, (c) modify sldRenderCanvas to add click handlers for fault injection when in sim mode (clicking any non-transformer node injects a fault), (d) when sldFaultSimResult is set, render the animated 3D fault visualization and results panel below the SLD canvas, (e) a 'Reset/Exit Simulation' button to clear the state.

STEP 7 - Farsi translations: Add all new user-facing strings to the _FA dictionary for complete trilingual support. Strings include: 'Fault Simulation', 'Short-circuit', 'Earth fault', 'Inject fault', 'Trip time', 'Required time', 'Protection adequate', 'Protection failed', 'Disconnection too slow', 'Selectivity issues', 'Device tripped', 'Fault current', 'Exit simulation', etc.

STEP 8 - Headless Tests: Add at least 8 new tests to test-sld.js covering: (a) sldGetTripTime returns correct time for MCB at known fault current, (b) sldGetTripTime returns Infinity when fault current is below device threshold, (c) sldSimulateFault returns valid result object with all required fields, (d) sldSimulateFault correctly identifies which device trips first on a multi-level tree, (e) sldSimulateFault correctly applies 0.4s requirement for final circuits <=32A, (f) sldSimulateFault correctly applies 5s requirement for distribution circuits, (g) sldSimulateFault detects selectivity issues (upstream device also trips), (h) sldRenderFaultAnimation returns valid SVG string, (i) all new functions are defined at global scope. Tests must be appended BEFORE the summary line in test-sld.js.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'node test-sld.js' passes all 39 existing tests plus at least 8 new fault simulation tests
sldSimulateFault(tree, nodeId, 'short_circuit') returns a complete result object with correct fault current matching sldCalcNodeIk
Trip time calculation uses conservative tMax values from device curves
DS/HD 60364-4-41 disconnection requirements are correctly applied (0.4s for final <=32A, 5s for distribution)
sldRenderFaultAnimation returns SVG with dark background, neon glow filter, and animation elements
The fault simulation mode is toggleable from the SLD view without typing
All user-facing strings support da/en/fa languages
Clicking a node in fault sim mode triggers fault injection rather than node selection
verification: Run 'node test-sld.js' and confirm all tests pass (39 existing + new tests)
Grep for 'sldSimulateFault' and 'sldGetTripTime' and 'sldRenderFaultAnimation' to confirm they are defined
Grep for '_FA' additions to confirm Farsi translations were added
